pi_items.source: Verified against task.json instructions and export_result.sh parsing logic.
Verified against task.json instructions and export_result.sh parsing logic.
Verified against task.json instructions and export_result.sh string matching criteria.

Code execution on the 50 sample emails loaded by setup_task.sh revealed exactly 0 emails containing any of the critical or warning keywords in their subjects. The claim of 5 relevant emails is impossible.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a sample of the real SpamAssassin Public Corpus (ham emails). The evaluation scripts explicitly require categorizing emails using critical keywords ('error', 'fail', 'failure', 'fatal') and warning keywords ('check', 'review', 'warning'), and sending a summary report to sre-leads@company.com. However, there is a critical defect: setup_task.sh loads exactly 50 sample emails into the inbox, and none of them contain any of these required keywords in their subjects. Consequently, it is impossible for the agent to accurately categorize 5 relevant emails as requested without failing the correctness checks.
